label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why Doyle was surprised. The story says that the driver asked him where to take him by name before Doyle even said anything. Doyle was surprised, so he asked if the driver had seen him before. The driver explained that he figured it out through clues from the newspaper, skin color, ink spot, clothes, etc. Then Doyle realized the driver was acting like Sherlock Holmes. The options are (A) the driver had seen him before, (B) the driver could call him by his name, (C) the driver was waiting for him, (D) the driver answered no questions from him. Doyle's surprise comes from the driver knowing his name and correctly deducing his identity without having met him before. The driver didn't actually see him before, as he said "No, sir, I have never seen you before." So (A) is incorrect. The driver called him by his name, which is why Doyle was surprised. So (B) seems correct. The driver wasn't waiting for him specifically, just at the taxi stop where
